---
title: "ickma.dev"
---

::: {.hero-banner}
# ickma.dev — Notes on Deep Learning and Math

A growing collection of structured study notes and visual explanations —
written for clarity, reproducibility, and long-term memory.
:::

## Latest Updates

::: {.section-block}
### ∇ Goodfellow Deep Learning Book <span class="section-count">64 chapters</span>

My notes on the Deep Learning book by Ian Goodfellow, Yoshua Bengio, and Aaron Courville.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[Chapter 20: Deep Generative Models](ML/deep-generative-models.qmd)**
Deep generative modeling spans energy-based models, directed latent-variable models, and implicit generators. This chapter surveys RBMs, DBNs/DBMs, VAEs, GANs, autoregressive models, and evaluation pitfalls.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Chapter 19: Approximate Inference](ML/approximate-inference.qmd)**
Exact posterior inference is intractable in deep latent models, so we optimize the ELBO instead. EM, MAP inference, and mean-field variational updates provide scalable approximations.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Chapter 18: Confronting the Partition Function](ML/confronting-partition-function.qmd)**
Energy-based models require a partition function for normalization. This chapter follows how $\nabla_\theta \log Z(\theta)$ enters the log-likelihood gradient and surveys training strategies like contrastive divergence, pseudolikelihood, score matching, NCE, and AIS that avoid or estimate $Z$.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Chapter 17: Monte Carlo Methods](ML/monte-carlo-methods.qmd)**
Monte Carlo estimation replaces intractable expectations with sample averages. Importance sampling reweights proposal draws to reduce variance, while Markov chain methods like Gibbs sampling generate dependent samples when direct sampling is infeasible. Tempering improves mixing across multimodal landscapes.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all Deep Learning chapters →](ML/deep-learning-book.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### ∇ CMU 10-414/714 Deep Learning Systems <span class="section-count">2 notes</span>

Course notes on deep learning systems, from ML foundations to training and deployment infrastructure.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[Lecture 3: Manual Neural Networks](ML/DLSys/cmu-dlsys-lecture-3-manual-neural-networks.qmd)**
Linear hypothesis limitations, feature maps, nonlinear activations, two-layer networks, ReLU, fully connected networks, and why depth helps in practice.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Lecture 2: ML Refresher / Softmax Regression](ML/DLSys/cmu-dlsys-lecture-2-ml-refresher-softmax-regression.qmd)**
Hypothesis classes, loss functions, optimization, softmax regression, cross-entropy, gradient descent, SGD, and the softmax gradient.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all CMU Deep Learning Systems notes ->](ML/DLSys/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### 📄 Papers in Deep Learning <span class="section-count">6 notes</span>

Paper reading notes that focus on key ideas, math intuition, and practical takeaways.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[Generative Adversarial Nets](ML/papers/generative-adversarial-nets.qmd)**
A minimax game between generator and discriminator, with the optimal discriminator derivation and the global optimum condition where generated and real distributions match.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Why TPU Is Fast for Dot Product (First-Gen TPU)](ML/papers/why-tpu-is-fast-for-dot-product.qmd)**
An early-TPU perspective (CNN/RNN era): focus on the systolic-array MMU to understand why ASIC specialization can greatly outperform general-purpose processors on matrix MAC inference workloads.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Transformer: Attention Is All You Need](ML/papers/transformer-attention-is-all-you-need.qmd)**
Attention-only sequence modeling with positional encoding, scaled dot-product attention, and multi-head projections for parallel long-range dependency learning.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Attention: The Origin of Transformer](ML/papers/attention-origin-transformer.qmd)**
Learnable alignment scores and a dynamic context vector replace the fixed encoder bottleneck in seq2seq models.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all Deep Learning papers →](ML/papers/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### ⚡ JAX <span class="section-count">4 notes</span>

Notes on the JAX ecosystem for machine learning, from function transformations and XLA compilation to Flax, Optax, Orbax, and Grain.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[Scaling Up](ML/JAX/scaling-up.qmd)**
Distributed training as a sharding problem: DDP, FSDP, tensor parallelism, JAX meshes, partition specs, sharded initialization, and NNX training loops.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[JAX NumPy](ML/JAX/jax-numpy.qmd)**
JAX NumPy keeps NumPy-like syntax while changing the execution model: JIT compilation, immutable arrays, explicit RNG keys, `vmap`, pytrees, and `shard_map`.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Introducing Flax NNX](ML/JAX/introducing-flax-nnx.qmd)**
Flax NNX gives JAX a stateful model API while keeping RNG streams, JIT compilation, gradients, and Optax updates explicit.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[JAX AI Stack](ML/JAX/jax-ai-stack.qmd)**
How JAX, XLA, Flax NNX, Optax, Orbax, and Grain fit together into a modern training stack, plus the role of jit, grad, and vmap.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all JAX notes →](ML/JAX/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### NLP <span class="section-count">4 notes</span>

Notes on natural language processing, from classical system-building approaches to modern neural methods.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[CMU Advanced NLP Lecture 16: Reinforcement Learning for LLMs](ML/NLP/cmu-advanced-nlp-lecture-16-reinforcement-learning-for-llms.qmd)**
RL for language models through one-step and token-level MDP views, policy gradient, group-based advantages, PPO, cold-start fine-tuning, verifiable rewards, math reasoning, and RLHF.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[CMU Advanced NLP Lecture 9: Decoding Algorithms](ML/NLP/cmu-advanced-nlp-lecture-9-decoding-algorithms.qmd)**
Greedy decoding, beam search, MAP pitfalls, sampling, top-k, top-p, temperature, and KV-cache speedups for language model generation.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[CMU Advanced NLP Lecture 2: Learned Representation](ML/NLP/cmu-advanced-nlp-lecture-2-learned-representation.qmd)**
Tokenization, token embeddings, CBOW, Deep CBOW, training losses, and a small experiment showing why depth after mean pooling does not recover word order.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[CMU Advanced NLP Lecture 1: Introduction and Fundamentals](ML/NLP/cmu-advanced-nlp-lecture-1-introduction-fundamentals.qmd)**
NLP system-building methods from rules to supervised learning and reinforcement learning, plus parameterization, learning, inference, softmax probabilities, and generation.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all NLP notes →](ML/NLP/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### RL <span class="section-count">11 notes</span>

Course notes on reinforcement learning, from classical RL foundations to language-model alignment.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[David Silver RL Course - Lecture 10: Classic Games](ML/RL/david-silver-lecture-10-classic-games.qmd)**
Classic games as RL case studies: game theory, minimax search, self-play TD learning, TD-Gammon, TreeStrap, Monte Carlo tree search, and imperfect-information games.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[David Silver RL Course - Lecture 9: Exploration and Exploitation](ML/RL/david-silver-lecture-9-exploration-and-exploitation.qmd)**
Exploration and exploitation through bandits, regret, epsilon-greedy, UCB, Bayesian bandits, Thompson Sampling, information-state search, contextual bandits, and MDPs.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[David Silver RL Course - Lecture 8: Integrating Learning and Planning](ML/RL/david-silver-lecture-8-integrating-learning-and-planning.qmd)**
Model-based reinforcement learning, learned models, planning with simulated experience, Dyna-Q, forward search, Monte Carlo tree search, TD search, and Dyna-2.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[David Silver RL Course - Lecture 7: Policy Gradient Methods](ML/RL/david-silver-lecture-7-policy-gradient-methods.qmd)**
Direct policy optimization with score functions, softmax and Gaussian policies, REINFORCE, actor-critic methods, baselines, advantage functions, eligibility traces, and natural policy gradients.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all RL notes →](ML/RL/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### ML HW-SW Codesign <span class="section-count">21 notes</span>

Notes on efficient AI systems where compression, deployment, and specialized hardware have to be designed together.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[Efficient AI Lecture 22: Quantum Machine Learning I](ML/HW-SW-codesign/efficient-ai-lecture-22-quantum-machine-learning-i.qmd)**
Quantum computing foundations for QML: qubits, measurement, Bloch sphere, gates, entanglement, quantum circuits, NISQ constraints, and qubit mapping.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Efficient AI Lecture 21: On-Device Training and Transfer Learning](ML/HW-SW-codesign/efficient-ai-lecture-21-on-device-training-transfer-learning.qmd)**
On-device learning for customization and privacy, gradient leakage risks, activation-memory bottlenecks, TinyTL, SparseBP, QAS, and PockEngine system support.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Efficient AI Lecture 20: Distributed Training (Part 2)](ML/HW-SW-codesign/efficient-ai-lecture-20-distributed-training-part-2.qmd)**
Auto-parallelization, distributed communication bottlenecks, sparse gradient compression, momentum correction, warm-up, PowerSGD, gradient quantization, and delayed gradient averaging.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Efficient AI Lecture 19: Distributed Training (Part 1)](ML/HW-SW-codesign/efficient-ai-lecture-19-distributed-training-part-1.qmd)**
Distributed training foundations: data, pipeline, tensor, and sequence parallelism; communication collectives; ZeRO/FSDP memory sharding; GPipe; and tensor-parallel Transformer layers.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[Efficient AI Lecture 18: Diffusion Models](ML/HW-SW-codesign/efficient-ai-lecture-18-diffusion-models.qmd)**
Diffusion models from noising and denoising to conditional generation, latent diffusion, DDIM, distillation, sparsity, quantization, and parallel sampling.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all ML HW-SW codesign notes →](ML/HW-SW-codesign/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### 🧪 Theory-to-Repro <span class="section-count">1 note</span>

Low-level ML understanding and paper reproduction through derivations and code.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[Linear Regression via Three Solvers](Theory-to-Repro/linear-regression-three-ways.qmd)**
Solve the same least-squares objective with pseudo-inverse, convex optimization, and SGD, then compare assumptions and scalability.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all Theory-to-Repro notes →](Theory-to-Repro/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

::: {.section-block}
### 📐 Math <span class="section-count">116 items</span>

Probability, information theory, quantum computing, calculus, linear algebra, and optimization notes organized as one mathematical foundation.

::: {.content-grid}

::: {.content-card}
**[MIT 6.041 Probability: Iterated Expectations](Math/Probability/iterated-expectations.qmd)**
Conditional expectation as a random variable, the law of iterated expectations, total variance, section-mean variance decomposition, and random sums.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[MIT 6.041 Probability: Derived Distributions and Covariance](Math/Probability/derived-distributions-covariance.qmd)**
Ratio distributions, infinite expectations, monotone transformations, convolution, sums of independent normals, covariance, variance of sums, and correlation.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[MIT 6.041 Probability: Continuous Bayes' Rule and Derived Distributions](Math/Probability/continuous-bayes-rule-derived-distributions.qmd)**
Continuous Bayes' rule, mixed discrete-continuous inference, derived distributions, CDF methods, and linear transformations of PDFs.
:::

::: {.content-card}
**[MIT 6.041 Probability: Multiple Continuous Random Variables](Math/Probability/multiple-continuous-random-variables.qmd)**
Joint PDFs, marginalization, independence, conditional densities, Buffon's needle, and a two-step stick-breaking example.
:::

:::

::: {.see-all-button}
[See all Math notes →](Math/index.qmd){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

:::

---

::: {.footer-section}
## More Topics

::: {.footer-grid}

::: {.footer-card}
### Machine Learning
- [K-Means Clustering](ML/k_means_clustering.qmd)
- [Logistic Regression](ML/logistic_regression.qmd)
- [Axis Operations](ML/axis.qmd)
:::

::: {.footer-card}
### Algorithms
- [DP Regex](Algorithm/dp_regex.qmd)
:::

:::

:::
